authoritative_sources.title: Code of Federal Regulations, Title 9, Animal Welfare
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, or official guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Title 9 is the authoritative federal baseline for animal welfare obligations and handling requirements that constrain what an animal-production execution lane can safely advance.
used_for: state transitions tied to welfare risk

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This regulation defines traceability, preventive-control, and supplier-related record expectations that affect closure and release-aware claims in food-animal production workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Food Traceability Rule FAQ
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA traceability guidance is the authoritative explanation of one-stop traceability expectations for food-related lanes where lot and movement context changes safe execution decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: 40 CFR Part 122 - National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA NPDES requirements govern environmental conditions that can materially stop or alter animal-production throughput, particularly around runoff and discharge events.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Environmental risk gating for production exceptions
used_for: execution stop conditions for runoff or discharge risk

authoritative_sources.title: EPA Concentrated Animal Feeding Operations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA CAFO guidance is an authoritative operating reference for environmental permit-sensitive activities and informs when production must be held, corrected, or escalated.

authoritative_sources.title: USDA APHIS Animal Health and Disease Response
authoritative_sources.publisher: USDA Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APHIS is the primary federal authority for animal health response context, defining conditions where production execution must be paused or routed to clinical authority.

authoritative_sources.title: NOAA Climate and Seasonal Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NOAA seasonal outlooks are recognized public sources for weather and climate risk signals that shift viable production and facility capacity decisions in land- and animal-based operations.

authoritative_sources.title: USDA Natural Resources Conservation Service Resources
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NRCS is the authoritative public source for land-management and soil/water stewardship context that affects land-use and grazing assumptions in animal operations.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized standards body for interoperable identifiers and event semantics used across farm, transport, and traceability systems.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and lane-boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Normalize species, cycle phase, terrain and seasonal context, tenant scope, and minimum prerequisites before any completion recommendation is made.
source_classes: Primary regulators, statutes, or official guidance

workflow_stages.name: Readiness and dependency verification
workflow_stages.description: Collect farm, land, environmental, and traceability records and verify required states before moving a lot/pen or herd state forward.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Production lifecycle progression
workflow_stages.description: Advance the execution state only when prerequisites and evidence thresholds are met and blockers are explicit.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Exception and escalation handling
workflow_stages.description: Route environmental, welfare, food-safety, traceability conflict, and out-of-scope authority cases to adjacent owners or human review.
source_classes: Primary regulators, statutes, or official guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and closure
workflow_stages.description: Emit a completion or fulfillment record only with explicit evidence and no unresolved release, welfare, or environmental hold signals.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

artifacts: request intake packet, species, herd/lot identity, and lifecycle phase
pasture and field use maps, weather alerts, and land condition notes
movement, pen/lot genealogy, and traceability records
quality, hold, environmental, and incident records
program and permit artifacts for grant, stewardship, or disposal conditions
systems_of_record: program administration and grant record system

allowed: classify in-scope requests and lane boundaries for execution progression
advance lifecycle states only from evidence-backed readiness and dependency checks
produce completion, exception, escalation, and production-status artifacts with audit-ready traces
route veterinary, environmental, legal, or permit signoff requirements to adjacent specialists
escalate: any welfare, health, food-safety, or environmental hold signal that changes output safety
release or completion decisions tied to unresolved permits, approvals, or policy precedence
refuse: final permit, legal, policy override, or financial approval authority
claims to complete fulfillment without required evidence

domain_failure_modes: Progressing a lot or herd to completion while pasture, welfare, or environmental stop conditions remain unresolved.
Advancing production through unresolved traceability conflicts across farm and transport records.
Marking completion despite missing lot genealogy, holding, or environmental-release artifacts.
Allowing seasonal runoff or heat-risk conditions to be treated as routine operational noise.
Inferring veterinary, environmental, or permit authority from incomplete context and issuing final decisions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Seasonal, land, or traceability dependency gap
Request that implies agronomic, veterinary, or environmental signoff
Execution exception requiring rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities: State-level agricultural welfare, permitting, and recordkeeping frameworks vary; the lane must capture those tenant/jurisdiction hooks and escalate when differences are material.
Public sources define federal and sector baselines, while private tenant SOPs may impose stricter throughput and completion artifacts.
